title: Listwise Same-Lap Ranker With Query Groups
summary: Current work already uses within-lap heuristics, but public ranking docs suggest moving from pairwise/heuristic blends to true query-group losses.
rationale: Current work already uses within-lap heuristics, but public ranking docs suggest moving from pairwise/heuristic blends to true query-group losses. Treat each (Year, Race, LapNumber) as a query and learn which drivers should rank highest for imminent pit risk. This matches the competition structure better than independent binary rows.
implementation_hint: Train a separate expert with `qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber)` using XGBoost LTR or CatBoost querywise ranking. Start with binary relevance `PitNextLap`, then test graded relevance like 3=pit next lap, 2=pit within 2 laps, 1=pit within 3-4 laps, 0 otherwise. Blend its OOF ranks, not raw probabilities, into the existing ensemble.
expected_effect: Could add a low-correlation expert that improves ordering inside crowded same-lap decisions, where AUC is won or lost.
risk: Easy to leak if graded labels or querywise normalizations peek forward across folds; very small query groups may add noise.
